content:
It’s not what you think…

3 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Every day, Google alone processes an estimated 16 billion searches, roughly 190,000 queries every second. Buried in that flood of curiosity, small talk, and idle clicking is a small set of terms that dominate again and again. So what actually sits at the top?
The Simple Answer: YouTube
Across the major keyword-tracking platforms, Semrush, Ahrefs, and others that mine billions of search queries, one term consistently comes out on top: “YouTube.”
It draws an estimated 1.38 billion searches per month worldwide. That’s nearly double the volume of the runner-up. The reason is mostly behavioral: instead of typing a URL directly or opening an app, huge numbers of people simply type “YouTube” into Google and click the first result. It’s a search engine being used as a bookmark, at global scale.
The Rest of the Top Five
Right behind YouTube, the rankings tell a story about how the internet has shifted in just the last two years:

• YouTube — ~1.38 billion monthly searches

• ChatGPT — ~1.12 billion monthly searches

• Facebook — ~618 million monthly searches

• WhatsApp Web
